growth_records.cite: Hara, Takeshi et al. Interspecies metabolite transfer fuels the methionine metabolism of Fusobacterium nucleatum to stimulate volatile methyl mercaptan production. mSystems (2024). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00764-23. [PMC10878106]
growth_records.snippet: Fusobacterium nucleatum subsp. nucleatum ATCC25586 ... L-Methionine 477.71 ± 26.30 (ppm CH3SH). F. nucleatum gradually consumed methionine, about 50% in the substrate present during 12 h of incubation ... Glutamate present in the mCDM was f
growth_records.notes: Resting washed-cell suspension assay (OD600=1.0), NOT a growth culture; OD 'virtually unchanged' after 16 h (Table S2) so no growth_rate/doubling_time derivable (mu~0). CH3SH secretion 477.71+/-26.30 ppm is a headspace GC value at 5 mM L-methionine, pH 6.5, 16 h (Table 1); a comparable ~500 ppm plateau at 3.5-5.0 mM Met at pH 6.5 is shown in Fig 2A. Methionine uptake reported only as ~50% of the supplied substrate over 12 h (Fig 3); the exact starting methionine concentration for the Fig 3 time-course is not explicitly stated (mCDM was supplemented with 0.5-5.0 mM Met 'as necessary'), so an absolute mmol or mmol/gDW/h uptake rate cannot be computed without assumption; no gDW-per-OD conversion is given, precluding biomass-specific normalization. 13C5,15N-methionine tracing (10 mM, 6 h) was performed but reported as relative isotopomer abundances (m+n) over time, not net fluxes with units, so mfa_fluxes left empty.

growth_records.strain: F. nucleatum ATCC 25586 + S. gordonii DL1 WT

up.units: mM net over 12 h (inferred: 1.2 mM S. gordonii mono - 0.95 mM coculture)
up.method: UPLC; difference of ornithine released in S. gordonii monoculture vs coculture

sec.method: gas chromatography with flame photometric detector

growth_records.cite: Hara, Takeshi et al. Interspecies metabolite transfer fuels the methionine metabolism of Fusobacterium nucleatum to stimulate volatile methyl mercaptan production. mSystems (2024). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00764-23. [PMC10878106]
growth_records.snippet: cocultures of F. nucleatum and S. gordonii WT exhibited the highest level of consumption of methionine ... release of 1.2 and 0.95 mM ornithine, respectively ... suggesting an uptake of 0.25 mM ornithine by F. nucleatum ... approximately 2.
growth_records.notes: Coculture of two resting washed-cell suspensions (each OD600=1.0); no net growth reported (bacterial growth not significantly affected by ornithine addition, data not shown; OD unchanged). Net ornithine accumulation in coculture = 0.95 mM over 12 h -> 0.95/12 = 0.079 mM/h. Inferred F. nucleatum ornithine uptake = 1.2 mM (S. gordonii monoculture) - 0.95 mM (coculture) = 0.25 mM over 12 h, as stated verbatim by authors. CH3SH enhancement ~2.3-fold (0.5 mM Met) to ~3-fold (1 mM Met) vs monocultures; enhancement preserved in non-contact coculture (diffusible factor = ornithine). 13C5,15N-methionine intracellular tracing (CE-TOF-MS, 6 h) mapped fate of Met into methionine cycle and polyamine/salvage pathway but reported only relative isotopomer abundances (m+n), not net fluxes with units -> mfa_fluxes empty. Gene-expression: mgl/metK up 2.3-4.2-fold, metK/metH/metQ/metI up 4.0-5.6-fold in coculture (qPCR), consistent with enhanced Met uptake.

growth_records.medium: Chemically defined medium (based on van der Hoeven), glucose+amino-acid supplemented

yields.note: Values are per-g-cell-protein amounts (biomass-normalised), not per-gDW/h. Intracellular polyglucose (IP) 2.39 ug glucose/mg protein; NAD-specific GDH activity 8.87 GDH unit/mg protein.

growth_records.cite: Chew, Jactty et al. A proteomic investigation of Fusobacterium nucleatum alkaline-induced biofilms. BMC Microbiology (2012). DOI:10.1186/1471-2180-12-189. [PMC3478200]
growth_records.method: reported (dilution rate D=0.069/h set by 27 mL/h feed; generation time Tg=ln2/D=10 h stated); mu=D at steady state
growth_records.snippet: Table 2. Growth pH 7.4: Glucose utilisation 23.1 +/- 2.1 (mmol glucose g-1 cell protein); IP 2.39 +/- 0.12; Lactate 5.7 +/- 0.5; Formate 92.4 +/- 8.6; Acetate 59.4 +/- 6.5; Butanoate 63.0 +/- 5.1 (mmol g-1 cell protein); GDH 8.87 +/- 0.40.
growth_records.notes: Planktonic steady-state chemostat, pH 7.4, anaerobic, 37C. Medium pumped 27 mL/h giving imposed D=0.069/h; Tg=ln2/D=ln2/0.069=10.0 h (paper states 10 h); at chemostat steady state mu=D=0.069/h. Reported quantities are per g cell protein (Table 2 footnotes: glucose in mmol/g protein; acidic end-products lactate/formate/acetate/butanoate in mmol/g protein; IP in ug glucose/mg protein; GDH in unit/mg protein) - NOT time-normalised. Derived steady-state specific rates (q = mu x amount-per-biomass, valid because at steady state specific rate = mu x per-biomass content): q_glucose=0.069 x 23.1=1.59 mmol/g protein/h; q_lactate=0.069 x 5.7=0.393; q_formate=0.069 x 92.4=6.38; q_acetate=0.069 x 59.4=4.10; q_butanoate=0.069 x 63.0=4.35 mmol/g protein/h. gDW-basis conversion not possible (protein fraction of DW not reported). Base defined medium composition from van der Hoeven reference not fully restated in paper; listed components are the stated supplements. F. nucleatum ferments glutamate/histidine via 2-oxoglutarate pathway to acetate+butanoate and sugars to acetate/formate/lactate.

growth_records.medium: Chemically defined medium (based on van der Hoeven), glucose+amino-acid supplemented

up.method: chemostat glucose utilisation measurement, biofilm cells (Table 2)

yields.note: Per-g-cell-protein amounts (biomass-normalised), not per-gDW/h. Intracellular polyglucose (IP) 7.62 ug glucose/mg protein (~3-fold up vs pH 7.4); NAD-specific GDH activity 13.73 GDH unit/mg protein (~1.5-fold up). Roughly 3-fold increase in glucose utilisation and lactate production vs pH 7.4.

growth_records.cite: Chew, Jactty et al. A proteomic investigation of Fusobacterium nucleatum alkaline-induced biofilms. BMC Microbiology (2012). DOI:10.1186/1471-2180-12-189. [PMC3478200]
growth_records.method: medium feed maintained at D=0.069/h, but cells grow as surface-attached biofilm (biomass retained) so mu is not equal to D and specific biofilm growth rate is not reported
growth_records.snippet: Table 2. Growth pH 8.2: Glucose utilisation 65.9 +/- 7.2 (mmol glucose g-1 cell protein); IP 7.62 +/- 0.71; Lactate 18.3 +/- 1.9; Formate 131.2 +/- 11.6; Acetate 115.3 +/- 12.7; Butanoate 99.6 +/- 10.8 (mmol g-1 cell protein); GDH 13.73 +/-
growth_records.notes: Alkaline-induced biofilm, pH 8.2, anaerobic, 37C, same chemostat/medium as pH 7.4 record. Reported values are per g cell protein (Table 2 footnotes), NOT time-normalised, and NOT convertible to per-gDW/h specific rates because (a) biofilm biomass is retained so mu != D=0.069/h, and (b) protein fraction of DW not reported. Values retained as experimental biomass-normalised uptake/secretion. Relative to pH 7.4: glucose utilisation up ~2.85x (65.9/23.1), lactate up ~3.2x (18.3/5.7), indicating a metabolic shift toward lactate/anaerobic glycolysis to help maintain intracellular pH (lactic acid pKa 3.08 vs formic 3.75, acetic 4.75, butanoic 4.82). Base defined medium composition per van der Hoeven reference not fully restated; listed components are stated supplements.
